conbody.p: Overview: The Assistant Usage Overview dashboard helps the CDI Manager to view insights into utilization metrics for CDS Assistant so that they can analyze effectiveness and adoption of the CDS Assistant and find ways to improve CDS engagement and participation. The dashboard also helps you group data by week, month, quarter, and year and analyze trends over different time periods and analyze them more easily.
By default, the report displays data for last 3 calendar months based on the admission date.
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE Triage Triage and CDS Assistant Usage Assistant Usage Overview .

The Assistant Usage Overview dashboard displays the following:
KPI Cards: Codes Addressed, Codes Accepted, Codes Removed
Encounters with Suggestions
Codes Presented on CDS Assistant
Avg. Suggestions per Reviewed Encounter
CDS Assistant Codes Addressed
CDS Activity
Suggestions Clarified
conbody.note: All encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed' are considered.
conbody.p: On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For View By option, refer .
For filters on all pages, refer .
For Footnotes, refer .

entry.p: Displays the count and percentage of suggestions that were addressed by the CDS for the selected time period.
Format: Codes Addressed (% Codes Addressed) 76 (77%).
Codes Addressed: Displays the count of suggestions that were addressed by the CDS.
% Codes Addressed: Out of total suggestions presented on CDS Assistant, this metric displays the percentage of suggestions that were addressed by the CDS.
% Codes Addressed = (Count of suggestions addressed by the CDS / Total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant).

li.p: Suggestions addressed are those that are accepted/removed/CDS Attributed by the CDS.
Codes can be Accepted and Removed for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down.
Suggestion could be Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: Displays the count and percentage of codes accepted by the CDS for the selected time period.
Format: Codes Accepted (% Codes Accepted) 76 (77%).
Codes Accepted: Displays out of total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant, the count of codes accepted by the CDS.
% Codes Accepted: Out of the total codes, suggested codes presented on CDS assistant, the percentage of codes that were accepted by the CDS.
% Codes Accepted = (Total count of codes accepted by the CDS / Total count of codes suggested). This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

li.p: Codes can be accepted for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories and are available under the 'Accepted’ drop down.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down and this is included in this metric.

entry.p: Displays the count and percentage of codes removed by the CDS for the selected time period.
Format: Codes Removed (% Codes Removed) 76 (77%).
Codes Removed: Displays out of total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant, the count of codes rejected/removed by the CDS.
% Codes Removed: Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the percentage of codes that were rejected/removed by the CDS.
% Codes Removed = (Total count of codes removed by the CDS / Total count of codes suggested). This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
entry.note: Codes can be removed for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’, ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses' and ‘Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences’ categories and are available under the 'Removed’ drop down.

conbody.p: This chart allows report users to analyze count and percentage of encounters for which at least one AI suggestion was presented in CDS Assistant for the selected 'View By' option.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except ‘Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses and Documented Diagnoses by default. For data on Documented Procedures and Clinical Evidence, use the 'Assistant Category’ slicer.

entry.p: Displays out of total admissions/discharges, the percentage of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. Format: 78%.
% Encounters with Code Suggestions = (Count of encounters with at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant/Total count of discharges) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
entry.note: Suggestion could be Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: Total Encounters: Displays the total count of admissions/discharges for the selected time period.
Encounters with Code Suggestions: Displays the count of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant.
Reviewed Encounters with Code Suggestions: Displays the count of encounters with an initial review date/time that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant.

li.p: Encounters reviewed are the count of encounters that have an initial review date/time.

row.entry:
Displays out of total encounters reviewed, the percentage of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. Format: 78%.% Reviewed Encounters with Code Suggestions = (Count of encounters with an initial review date/time that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant/Total count of encounters reviewed) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
Displays total count of encounters reviewed are the count of encounters that have an initial review date/time.
Suggestion could be Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidence.

fig.title: Codes presented on CDS Assistant Chart

conbody.p: This chart allows report users to analyze total volume and view trend of codes suggested in CDS Assistant for the selected 'View By' option.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses and Documented Diagnoses by default. For data on Documented Procedures and Clinical Evidence, use the 'Assistant Category’ slicer.

table.title: Codes presented on CDS Assistant Metrics

entry.p: Displays the total count of AI suggestion codes presented on CDS Assistant for the selected View By option. Suggestion could be Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidence.

fig.title: Avg. Suggestions per Reviewed Encounter Chart

conbody.p: This chart allows to analyze average number of CLU suggestions presented for a reviewed encounter for the selected 'View By' option.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses and Documented Diagnoses by default. For data on Documented Procedures and Clinical Evidence, use the 'Assistant Category’ slicer.

table.title: Avg. Suggestions per Reviewed Encounter Metrics

entry.p: Displays the average count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant for every encounter that has an initial review date/time assigned to it.

Suggestions per Reviewed encounter = Count of all suggestions (Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidence) presented on CDS Assistant for all encounters with an initial review date/time assigned to it.
Avg. Suggestions per Reviewed Encounter = (Sum of all suggestions presented on CDS Assistant for all reviewed encounters/Total Reviewed Encounters).

li.p: Displays total count of encounters reviewed are the count of encounters that have an initial review date/time.
Suggestion could be Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidence.

conbody.p: This chart enables report users to analyze the utilization of AI suggestions provided within CDS Assistant.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses and Documented Diagnoses by default. For data on Documented Procedures and Clinical Evidence, use the 'Assistant Category’ slicer.

entry.p: Out of total suggestions presented on CDS Assistant, this metric displays the percentage of suggestions that were addressed by the CDS.
% Codes Addressed = (Count of suggestions addressed by the CDS / Total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant).

li.p: Suggestions addressed are those that are accepted/removed/CDS Attributed by the CDS.
Codes can be accepted and removed for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down.
Suggestion could be Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: Codes Addressed: Displays the count of suggestions that were addressed by the CDS.

conbody.p: This chart allows report users to analyze acceptance and rejection rate of AI suggestion and the trend of non-utilization.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses and Documented Diagnoses by default. For data on Documented Procedures and Clinical Evidence, use the 'Assistant Category’ slicer.

entry.p: Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the count of codes that were accepted by the CDS for the selected 'View By' option.

li.p: Codes can be Accepted for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories and are available under the 'Accepted’ drop down.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down and this is included in this metric.

entry.p: Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the count of codes that were rejected/removed by the CDS for the selected 'View By' option.
entry.note: Codes can be removed for ‘Documented Diagnoses', 'Documented Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses' and ‘Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences’ categories and are available under the 'Removed’ drop down.

entry.p: Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the count of codes that were ‘CDS Attributed’ by the CDS for the selected 'View By' option.
entry.note: Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down and this is included in this metric.

entry.p: Out of the total codes, suggested codes presented on CDS Assistant, the count of codes that were unaddressed or no action was taken by the CDS for the selected 'View By' option.

conbody.p: This chart allows report users to analyze percentage of AI Suggestions that were also clarified by the CDS, i.e. out of the total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant, the percentage of codes for which CDS raised a query in CDE One.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses and Documented Diagnoses by default. For data on Documented Procedures and Clinical Evidence, use the 'Assistant Category’ slicer.

entry.p: Displays the out of total suggestions that were presented on CDS Assistant, the percentage of codes for which CDS generated a clarification.
% Suggestions Clarified = (Total count of suggestions for which CDS generated a query / Total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant) X100; round off to 0 decimal places.

li.p: Deleted, Retracted and Failed clarifications are excluded by default.

Clarifications will only be generated for the Suggested codes within Documented Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, i.e. Clarifications are only created for codes with Suggestion Type = Suggested.
Any clarification that was unsent, retracted, deleted or failed is excluded.

row.entry: Suggestions Clarified: Display the total count of suggestions for which CDS presented/sent a clarification.

When this option is selected, all charts in the dashboard display the data by month of admission in ascending order. When you click this option, Group By and Week Start Day filters automatically become available for selection.
When the ‘View By’ is Admit Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by Admit month in ascending order.Admit Month is selected as the default 'View By' option.

entry.p: When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by month of discharge in ascending order. When you click this option, Group By and Week Start Day filters automatically become available for selection.
When the ‘View By’ is Discharge Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by Discharge month in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by facility in ascending order.
When the ‘View By’ is Facility, the data in all the charts is sorted by Facility name in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by payer in ascending order.
When the ‘View By’ is Payer, the data in all the charts is sorted by Payer in ascending order.

row.entry:
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by CDS.
When the ‘View By’ is CDS, the data in all the charts is sorted by CDS name in ascending order.

entry.p: When the ‘Assistant Category’ option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by different Assistant Categories.
When the ‘View By’ is Assistant Category, the data in all the charts is sorted by Assistant Category name in ascending order.

row.entry:
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by the highest rank assigned to the encounter. All ranks (both standard and custom ranks) are displayed.
When the ‘View By’ is Highest Rank, the data in all the charts is sorted by Highest Rank in ascending order.Encounters that do not have a rank assigned are grouped under the 'Unranked' category.

li.p: Allows user to select the admit date range.
Format: Between date range, example: 4/1/2023 - 6/30/2023.

ul.li: Default: Last 3 calendar months date range selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range.

li.p: Format: Between date range, example: 4/1/2023 - 6/30/2023.

ul.li: With the Reset button, you can reset both the admit and discharge date range.
Tooltip: Ctrl + Click to reset both Admit Date and Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID).
Default: ALL.
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID).
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays both active and inactive CDSs in alphabetical order associated with the facilities user has access to.
User can select multiple CDSs
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Users can search the CDS by CDS ID or Name.
Format: CDS Name (CDS ID).
Default: ALL.

ul.li: Displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays the list of Assistant Categories available in CDS Assistant.
Example: Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, DO: Diagnoses, DO: Clinical Evidence.
User can select multiple Assistant categories.
Users can search the Assistant Category.
Format: Assistant Category.
Default: DO: Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses.
Displays the list of different activities that can be performed by the CDS.
Example: Accepted, Removed, Unaddressed, CDS Attributed.
User can select multiple activities.
Users can search the CDS Activity.
Format: CDS Activity.
Default: ALL.
Displays the list of different suggestion types available within CDS Assistant.
Example: Suggested, Quality, Other, System Attributed.
User can select multiple Suggestion Types.
Users can search the Suggestion Type.
Format: Suggestion Type.
Default: Suggested.
Displays all standard and custom ranks that can be assigned to the encounters.
User can select multiple ranks.
Users can search the rank number.
“Unranked” displays data for all encounters that are not ranked.
Format: Rank # ( such as Rank 1, Rank 2).
Default: ALL.

row.entry:
These options are available only when the View By option is selected as Admit Date/Discharge Date.
See .

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL

li.p: Displays the available options for Weekend Only Stay: Y and N.
User can select more than one option.
Weekend Only Stay = Y (Unassigned encounters admitted after Friday, 6PM and Discharged before Sunday, 11.59 PM).
Default: ALL.

row.entry: The count of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. % Encounters with Code Suggestions = Encounters with Code Suggestions / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.

The count of reviewed encounters the had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. % Reviewed Encounters with Code Suggestions = Reviewed Encounters with Code Suggestions / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.
The count of AI suggestions presented in CDS Assistant.

The average number of AI suggestions presented for a reviewed encounter. Avg. Suggestions per Reviewed Encounter = Codes Presented on CDS Assistant/ Encounters Reviewed E.g., 11; Encounters Reviewed: The count of encounters reviewed with or without clarifications.

The count of AI suggestions that were addressed by the CDS. Addressing a code includes accepting or removing the code or attributing the Clinical Evidence. % Codes Addressed = Codes Addressed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were accepted by the CDS in CDS Assistant. % Accepted = Accepted/ Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were removed by the CDS in CDS Assistant. % Removed = Removed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.

The count of Clinical Evidence suggestions that were attributed by the CDS in CDS Assistant. % CDS Attributed = CDS Attributed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were unaddressed by the CDS. % Unaddressed = Codes Unaddressed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were clarified by the CDS. % Suggestions Clarified =Suggestions Clarified/ Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 10%.
